content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     About us     Sustainability     Disaster relief and support   Disaster relief and support   NAB Ready Together helps Australians withstand and recover from natural disasters.    Get help now     If you need disaster or crisis support, we can help.     Get support   NAB Ready Together   As Australians face more frequent natural disasters, there’s a growing need to help communities become more resilient.    We’re playing our part through NAB Ready Together, opens in new window    . This NAB and NAB Foundation flagship program helps Australians withstand and recover from natural disasters. When you’re threatened by bushfires, floods or cyclones, we'll be there.     Whatever comes, we’re ready together.         Disaster relief partnerships proudly supported by NAB Foundation    It takes many hands to get communities back up and running after a disaster. That’s why NAB and NAB Foundation partner with disaster relief organisations to help communities recover faster.    Disaster Relief Australia    Disaster Relief Australia, opens in new window    (DRA) is a veteran-led not-for-profit that unites veterans and volunteers from all walks of life to help communities prepare for and recover from disaster.    Unlike emergency services, which provide immediate disaster response, DRA focuses on either side of the emergency in the preparedness and recovery phases. It works with communities to identify risks, develop disaster management strategies, and build their capability to face future challenges. When disaster strikes, DRA shifts its focus to recovery. In the aftermath of a disaster, DRA provides essential cleanup and support to help communities pick up the pieces and rebuild their lives. Be it flood, bushfire, cyclone or storm, DRA is there to support people on some of their toughest days, helping them take the first steps on the road to recovery.    A founding partner since May 2023, NAB Foundation supports DRA to bolster corporate and community volunteerism through the NAB Foundation Recovery Crew. In 2024, 470 corporate and community volunteers were deployed to 12 operations around Australia, including 237 NAB colleagues.    Girls on Fire    Girls on Fire, opens in new window    is a not-for-profit organisation driving change in the emergency services sector. With continued support from the NAB Foundation, Girls on Fire have expanded their fire and resilience programs across Australia, empowering more young people to step forward and be leaders in their community.    The programs aim to train the next generation of firefighters and improve gender diversity and inclusion in the emergency sector. During a Girls on Fire resilience camp, facilitators take participants through hands-on practical rotations to help young girls and gender diverse kids build resilience and show them what leadership under pressures looks like.    “Everyone has a role to play in protecting Australia from disasters. That’s why we’re focused on inspiring the next generation of young girls and gender diverse kids.    It’s incredible and with NAB Foundation’s continued support and the signing of a new three-year partnership I’ve been able to reach more young people across Australia,” says Bronnie Mackintosh, CEO and Founder of Girls on Fire.    Learn more about our partnership with Girls on Fire, opens in new window    .    Disaster relief partnerships proudly supported by NAB   Resilient Building Council    Everyone has the right to safe, healthy, and secure housing. Resilient Building Council’s, opens in new window     mission is a resilience revolution - where better housing for all contributes to thriving, sustainable communities.    NAB was the only bank to partner with Resilient Building Council to develop tools to help customers retrofit their homes to be more bushfire resilient in 2022.     NAB supported the development of RBC’s Bushfire Resilience Rating system and self-assessment app, opens in new window    . The app measures the resilience of individual homes to bushfire risk and provides tailored recommendations for homeowners to improve their rating. The higher the properties’ risk, the greater the bushfire peril component of the insurance premium - and therefore, the higher the potential savings. To date, households in the highest risk areas have achieved annual premium reductions of up to $800 through verified Resilience Ratings. Find out more about participating insurers in the Bushfire Resilience Ratings Scheme, opens in new window    .    Complete your free Bushfire Resilience Rating Home self-assessment, opens in new window    and receive a tailored report to improve your resilience.    To date, over 46,000 households from 324 bushfire prone local government areas have accessed the app (as at 21 January 2025).    Learn more about our partnership with Resilient Building Council, opens in new window    .    Get help in a crisis    We offer support for customers impacted by a natural disaster by providing direct cash grants to help customers impacted by a natural disaster.    Find out what support and financial assistance we can provide if you’re being impacted by a natural disaster.     “A lot of people are scared of contacting the bank when they are having financial difficulties … They don’t realise how much we can help, if they can just work with us.”    Chantelle Marianov, NAB Assist banker     Helping you in times of crisis    We’re helping Australians recover from and build resilience to natural disasters.    Learn more about disaster relief support. , opens in new window   Other ways we can support you    Find out how NAB Foundation is supporting projects that help people and communities withstand natural disasters, and how to prepare for an emergency.     NAB Foundation Community Grants   Learn how to apply for a Community Grant and help your local community prepare for and recover from natural disasters.    NAB Foundation Environmental Resilience Grants   The Environmental Resilience Fund supports practical projects that build resilience to natural disasters and climate change.     Our volunteering and giving program   Find out more about volunteering and giving at NAB.    Climate change   We’re taking steps to address climate change.    Whatever happens, be prepared   Make sure your home and family are ready for a natural disaster.   Still need some help?   Easy ways to get in touch for support.    Call our NAB Assist team   Monday to Friday, 8:00am to 8:00pm (AEST/AEDT), Saturday, 9:00am to 1:00pm (AEST/AEDT)    Within Australia: 1300 661 114    From overseas: +61 3 9322 7000    Use our virtual assistant   Our virtual assistant can help answer your general enquiries or transfer you to a specialist.     Select the red Chat bubble in the bottom corner of your screen.
